page: Google Cloud Platform c2d-highcpu-4 is a compute-optimized virtual machine instance powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 2 physical cores with multithreading enabled. The system is equipped with 8.0 GB of system memory, providing a 2.0 GB per vCPU ratio. The underlying x86_64 architecture includes 32 KB L1d cache, 0.5 MB L2 cache, and 32 MB L3 cache. No local storage or GPU accelerators are bundled with this instance.

Benchmark results show strong single-core CPU performance, with stress-ng single-core tests placing in the top 25% at 2924.48. However, multi-core performance is relatively weak, with stress-ng multi-core and Geekbench multi-core scores falling into the bottom 25%. Memory performance is mixed; large-block read and write bandwidth tests show average performance, but the PassMark memory score is poor, ranking in the bottom 10%. Cryptographic operations perform moderately well, supported by hardware-accelerated AES and VAES instruction sets.

Given its dedicated CPU allocation and compute-focused resource density, the c2d-highcpu-4 offers a cost-efficient entry point for workloads requiring dedicated AMD EPYC execution threads without large memory footprints. The instance is suited for single-threaded processing tasks, lightweight web serving, basic text processing, and small-scale LLM inference text generation. It is less suitable for memory-intensive databases, parallelized compilation, or heavy multi-threaded workloads due to its constrained memory capacity and lower multi-core benchmark scaling.
description: Google Cloud Platform c2d-highcpu-4 is a compute-optimized instance featuring 4 dedicated vCPUs and 8.0 GB of RAM, powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor on the x86_64 architecture. It provides 2 physical cores with multithreading, 32 MB of L3 cache, and lacks local storage or GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks indicate strong single-core CPU execution, ranking in the top 25% in stress-ng tests, alongside moderate performance in large-block memory bandwidth and cryptographic operations. However, multi-core scaling and overall memory performance are relatively weak, with the PassMark memory score falling into the bottom 10%. This resource profile makes the instance a cost-efficient option for compute-bound, single-threaded tasks, lightweight web serving, and basic text processing, while it remains less optimal for memory-heavy databases or highly parallelized workloads.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c2d-highcpu-4 is a compute-optimized instance with 4 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs and 8 GB RAM, offering strong single-core performance for lightweight, compute-focused workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c2d-highcpu-4 is a compute-optimized instance featuring 4 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 8 GB RAM, and strong single-core performance.
tagline: A compute-optimized virtual server featuring dedicated AMD EPYC cores, delivering strong single-thread performance for resource-focused workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by AMD EPYC 7B13 processor with 4 dedicated vCPUs
Equipped with 8.0 GB of RAM (2.0 GB per vCPU)
Strong single-core CPU performance in stress-ng benchmarks
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
Best suited for single-threaded tasks and lightweight web serving
